title: Ramkeli Madanmohan Jiu temple
tagline: Ramkeli Madanmohan Jiu temple — spiritual town of West Bengal

heroImage.alt: Ramkeli Madanmohan Jiu temple, West Bengal

overview.short: Ramkeli Madanmohan Jiu Mandir is a Vaishnava temple at Ramkeli village in West Bengal.
overview.description: Ramkeli Madanmohan Jiu Mandir is a Vaishnava temple at Ramkeli village in West Bengal. Hindu god Krishna is worshiped in the form of Madanamohan in this temple. It is located in the ruined medieval city of Gauda in Malda district. Built by Rupa Goswami and Sanatana Goswami, known as Mahavaishnavas, the temple stands in the northern part of the village.

topPlaces.name: Ramkeli Mela
topPlaces.description: Ramkeli Mela (Bengali: রামকেলি মেলা) is a pilgrimage and fair of Hinduism. On the occasion of this fair, devout Hindus come to Ramkeli.

topPlaces.name: Lattan Mosque
topPlaces.description: The Lattan Mosque (Bengali: লোটন মসজিদ) or Painted Mosque, officially known as Lottan Masjid is a historical mosque located between the Tantipara Mosque and the Bridge of Five Arches in Gaur, Malda District, West Bengal. Built during the period of Hussain Shahi sultans between (c. 1493-1519), the mosque stands out as one of the best preserved monuments in ancient city of Gaur.

topPlaces.name: Baro Shona Masjid
topPlaces.description: The Baro Shona Masjid (Bengali: বড়ো সোনা মসজিদ, romanized: Great Golden Mosque), also known as the Baroduari Masjid (Bengali: বারোদুয়ারী মসজিদ, romanized: 12-gated Mosque) and as the Qutub Shahi Mosque, is a former mosque in a partial ruinous state, located in Gour, in the Malda district, in the state of West Bengal, India. Completed in 1526 CE, it is situated 500 metres (1,600 ft) south of Ramkeli, a village on the way to Gour, and 12 kilometres (7.5 mi) south of the town of Malda.

topPlaces.name: Dakhil Darwaza
topPlaces.description: Dakhil Darwaza, (Bengali: দাখিল দরওয়াজা, lit. 'Main gate') also known as Salami Darwaza, is a grand 15th-16th century gateway located in Gaur, Malda district, West Bengal, India. It served as the main northern entrance to the citadel of Lakhnauti, the capital of the Bengal Sultanate.

topPlaces.name: Qadam Rasul Mosque
topPlaces.description: The Qadam Rasul Mosque (Bengali: কদম রসুল মসজিদ) built in 1530-1531 by Sultan Nusrat Shah (r. 1519–1533) in the ancient city of Gaur, is a historic single-domed mosque known for housing a stone tablet bearing the footprint of Prophet Muhammad. The mosque, set amidst a lush garden, is a significant heritage and cultural site, maintained by the Archaeological Survey of India.

topPlaces.name: Lukochuri Darwaza
topPlaces.description: Lukochuri Darwaza, (Bengali: লুকোচুরি দরওয়াজা, lit. 'Hide and Seek Gate,') is a historic Mughal-era gateway in Gaur, Malda, West Bengal, India. It is situated on north of Gumti Gate and southeast of the Qadam Rasul Mosque.

topPlaces.name: Gumti Darwaza
topPlaces.description: The Gumti Darwaza or Gumti Gate (Bengali: গুমটি দরওয়াজা) is a historical monument located in the city of Gour, Malda, West Bengal, India, serving as the eastern gateway to the citadel of Gaur-Lakhnauti, the capital of the Bengal Sultanate. Assumed to be built in 1512 by Alauddin Hussain Shah, it is a fine example of Sultanate architecture, distinct from Mughal structures like the nearby Lukochuri Darwaza.

topPlaces.name: Bengal Sultanate
topPlaces.description: The Bengal Sultanate was a late medieval sultanate based in the Bengal region in eastern South Asia between the 14th and 16th century. It was the dominant power of the Ganges-Brahmaputra Delta, with a network of mint towns spread across the region.

items.note: Ramkeli Mela (Bengali: রামকেলি মেলা) is a pilgrimage and fair of Hinduism. On the occasion of this fair, devout Hindus come to Ramkeli.

The Lattan Mosque (Bengali: লোটন মসজিদ) or Painted Mosque, officially known as Lottan Masjid is a historical mosque located between the Tantipara Mosque and the Bridge of Five Arches in Gaur, Malda District, West Bengal. Built during the period of Hussain Shahi sultans between (c. 1493-1519), the mosque stands out as one of the best preserved monuments in ancient city of Gaur.

The Baro Shona Masjid (Bengali: বড়ো সোনা মসজিদ, romanized: Great Golden Mosque), also known as the Baroduari Masjid (Bengali: বারোদুয়ারী মসজিদ, romanized: 12-gated Mosque) and as the Qutub Shahi Mosque, is a former mosque in a partial ruinous state, located in Gour, in the Malda district, in the state of West Bengal, India. Completed in 1526 CE, it is situated 500 metres (1,600 ft) south of Ramkeli, a village on the way to Gour, and 12 kilometres (7.5 mi) south of the town of Malda.

Dakhil Darwaza, (Bengali: দাখিল দরওয়াজা, lit. 'Main gate') also known as Salami Darwaza, is a grand 15th-16th century gateway located in Gaur, Malda district, West Bengal, India. It served as the main northern entrance to the citadel of Lakhnauti, the capital of the Bengal Sultanate.

The Qadam Rasul Mosque (Bengali: কদম রসুল মসজিদ) built in 1530-1531 by Sultan Nusrat Shah (r. 1519–1533) in the ancient city of Gaur, is a historic single-domed mosque known for housing a stone tablet bearing the footprint of Prophet Muhammad. The mosque, set amidst a lush garden, is a significant heritage and cultural site, maintained by the Archaeological Survey of India.

Lukochuri Darwaza, (Bengali: লুকোচুরি দরওয়াজা, lit. 'Hide and Seek Gate,') is a historic Mughal-era gateway in Gaur, Malda, West Bengal, India. It is situated on north of Gumti Gate and southeast of the Qadam Rasul Mosque.

The Gumti Darwaza or Gumti Gate (Bengali: গুমটি দরওয়াজা) is a historical monument located in the city of Gour, Malda, West Bengal, India, serving as the eastern gateway to the citadel of Gaur-Lakhnauti, the capital of the Bengal Sultanate. Assumed to be built in 1512 by Alauddin Hussain Shah, it is a fine example of Sultanate architecture, distinct from Mughal structures like the nearby Lukochuri Darwaza.

The Bengal Sultanate was a late medieval sultanate based in the Bengal region in eastern South Asia between the 14th and 16th century. It was the dominant power of the Ganges-Brahmaputra Delta, with a network of mint towns spread across the region.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Ramkeli Madanmohan Jiu temple?
faq.a: The best time to visit Ramkeli Madanmohan Jiu temple is Oct – Mar. Summers are 25–42°C and winters are 7–24°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Ramkeli Madanmohan Jiu temple?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Bagdogra Airport (Siliguri/Darjeeling) (200 km) and the nearest railway station is Katihar Junction (91 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Ramkeli Madanmohan Jiu temple?
faq.a: 3 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Ramkeli Madanmohan Jiu temple at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Ramkeli Madanmohan Jiu temple?
faq.a: Stays in Ramkeli Madanmohan Jiu temple start from ₹900 per night, with options across 4 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Ramkeli Madanmohan Jiu temple?
faq.a: The top attractions are Ramkeli Mela, Lattan Mosque, Baro Shona Masjid, Dakhil Darwaza, Qadam Rasul Mosque.
